pdf.title: Verus_Tutorial_and_Reference
pdf.snippet: In our implementation, as usual, we tie the concrete result r to our spec in the ensures clause. Note that we use an i128 to compute the account’s running sum since it allows us to have suﬃciently large numbers without overﬂowing. As an exercise, you can try modifying the implementation to use an i64 for the sum instead, adding any additional invariants and proofs you need. Here we use a for loop instead of a while loop, which means ...

tutorial.title: Requires and ensures with mutable references
tutorial.snippet: # Requires and ensures with mutable references If a function takes a mutable reference, say `x: &mut T`, as an argument, then function specification needs to be able to refer to _two_ different value: the value behind the mutable reference _before_ the function executes, and the value _after_.
